raw_excerpt: {"binding_class":"A","input":"236HK","move_group":"specials","move_name":"Tiger Knee Crush","notes_sc":"Furthest horizontal distance; much better block advantage when spaced at max range; 2nd hit active frames 1-9 whiff on crouching opponents; this means that if the Knee is spaced out so the first hit whiffs, it connects on the 2nd to last active frame vs. crouching opponents (+4 on block); if the 1st hit is crouch blocked, opponent cannot switch to stand block the 2nd hit; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (in the corner, can juggle 623LP if the last 6 active frames connect, or 623MP / SA2 if the final active frame connects); blocked Knee causes a screen shake when Sagat has frame advantage; Sweet Spot occurs when juggling into the 3rd or 4th active frame, dealing 1300 damage (single hit) with a higher spinning juggle launch (ensures a follow-up juggle is possible even midscreen); the 2nd hit's extended Knee hitbox is too high to connect on standing characters on active frames 2-7 (tall characters: active frames 4-6); sets up corner safe jump in certain juggle scenarios (e.g. after juggled 2MP~HP)","punish_adv":"KD +40~41 (KD +37~47)","raw_source_token":"sagat_236hk"}

{"binding_class":"A","input":"236KK","move_group":"specials","move_name":"Tiger Knee Crush","notes_sc":"1st hit transitions to a different follow-up on hit, resulting in higher damage and a tumbling +66 knockdown (allows a 623LP juggle in the corner); if 1st hit whiffs, the next 2 hits deal 500 damage each and give a worse knockdown (only 1 hit at farthest ranges); safe on block but usually lands inside the opponent's throw range; 2nd hit active frames 2-10 whiff on crouching opponents; this means that if the Knee is spaced out, it connects on the last active frame vs. crouching opponents (+1 on block); unlike HK Knee, the opponent can switch to stand block after the first hit connects, leaving Sagat at -2; blocked Knee causes a screen shake when Sagat has frame advantage; later hits put opponent into a limited juggle state (in the corner, can juggle 623LP)","punish_adv":"KD +66 Tumble (KD +44~50)","raw_source_token":"sagat_236kk"}

{"binding_class":"A","input":"214HK","move_group":"specials","move_name":"Tiger Nexus","notes_sc":"Slow startup, only combos naturally from the first hit of 5HK (requires Drive Rush or Punish Counter to combo from 2HP, 4HP launch, or 5HK 2nd hit); 214HK cannot frame trap when canceled from a normal (except for Burnout + Drive Rush into 2MP/2HP/5HK); combos naturally into all follow-ups including 6HK; cancel into 6HK follow-up leaves a 7f gap on block (can frame trap 4f normals in Burnout); 2f extra recovery on Perfect Parry only; if the 1st hit is Perfect Parried, 2nd hit will interrupt any punish slower than 5f startup (trades with 6f attacks); cannot perform follow-up attacks if both hits whiff; carries Counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage through both hits; can juggle after DR~4HP (easier execution but less damaging than his MP Tiger Shot juggle sequence); opponents can beat all follow-ups with invincible reversals (but standard 6f reversals will not punish 214HK)","punish_adv":"+8","raw_source_token":"sagat_214hk"}

{"binding_class":"A","input":"214KK","move_group":"specials","move_name":"Tiger Nexus","notes_sc":"Similar to HK version but faster (can cancel from more starters like 2HP, 4HP launch, and 5HK 2nd hit); combos naturally into all follow-ups including 6HK; cancel into 6HK follow-up leaves a 7f gap on block (can frame trap 4f normals in Burnout); 2f extra recovery on Perfect Parry only; if the 1st hit is Perfect Parried, 2nd hit will interrupt any punish slower than 5f startup (trades with 6f attacks); cannot perform follow-up attacks if both hits whiff; carries Counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage through both hits; opponents can beat all follow-ups with invincible reversals (but standard 6f reversals will not punish 214KK)","punish_adv":"+8","raw_source_token":"sagat_214kk"}

{"binding_class":"E","input":"214214K","move_group":"super_arts","move_name":"Savage Tiger","notes_sc":"Goes into one of 4 follow-ups after frame 76 on hit (depending on which direction is held while clutching the opponent); Sagat's best reversal Super (but is still slow enough to be baited by an opponent's safejab setup); can be used as a projectile counter from about half screen; Sagat has travel time throughout the active frames (takes longer than SA3 to reach full range)","punish_adv":"HKD ~","raw_source_token":"sagat_214214k"}

{"binding_class":"E","input":"214214K~5","move_group":"super_arts","move_name":"Savage Tiger Raid (Damage)","notes_sc":"Highest damage variant of SA2; leaves the opponent far away with no oki; has significant corner carry (over 1/2 screen)","punish_adv":"HKD +4","raw_source_token":"sagat_214214k_5"}

{"binding_class":"D","input":"214KK~6HK","move_group":"nova_tiger","move_name":"Nova Tiger","notes_sc":"Connects on both standing/crouching opponents; launches opponent into a limited juggle state; combos naturally from 214KK; after blocked 214KK, interruptible 7f blockstring gap (frame trap vs. Burnout); unsafe to reversal Drive Impact and susceptible to Perfect Parry on reaction (becomes DI-safe from 214KK if opponent delays it by 2f or longer); opponent is left inside throw range on block (but Sagat cannot shimmy an immediate throw tech)","punish_adv":"KD +62","raw_source_token":"sagat_214kk_6hk"}

{"binding_class":"D","input":"214KK~6LK","move_group":"mighty_tiger","move_name":"Mighty Tiger","notes_sc":"Mixup tool that is unsafe on block; the threat of overhead makes ~6MK follow-up stronger while the threat of a frame trap makes ~6HK follow-up stronger; safe vs. Burnout but no longer frame traps, making it a weak option; puts airborne opponents into a limited juggle state (e.g. after DI wallsplat juggle); causes immediate knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214KK unless the 6LK input is delayed","punish_adv":"KD +25","raw_source_token":"sagat_214kk_6lk"}

{"binding_class":"D","input":"214KK~6MK","move_group":"greedy_tiger","move_name":"Greedy Tiger","notes_sc":"Whiffs on crouching opponents; 2f extra recovery on whiff; puts opponent into a limited juggle state (only allows 623LP/PP juggle in the corner); frame data vs. crouch from 214KK: -17 oH / -24 oB / -13 onPC; safe to reversal Drive Impact after blocked 214KK; 3f blockstring gap after 214KK (frame trap vs. standing normals but still loses to reversal crouching normals); risky to attempt on block, but the threat of the overhead and frame trap from ~6LK follow-up can condition opponents to stand block or parry; opponent cannot walk back out of throw range on block","punish_adv":"KD +48 (-13 crouch)","raw_source_token":"sagat_214kk_6mk"}

{"binding_class":"D","input":"5HP~HK","move_group":"target_combos","move_name":"Tiger Sting","notes_sc":"Both hits are always a true blockstring; hitconfirmable knockdown from a very strong midrange poke; sets up a KD +42 safe jump when juggled into (like after a DI wallsplat on hit/block)","punish_adv":"KD +39","raw_source_token":"sagat_5hp_hk"}

{"binding_class":"D","input":"MPMK","move_group":"system","move_name":"or 66 Drive Rush Cancel","notes_sc":"See Drive Rush ; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties","punish_adv":"-","raw_source_token":"sagat_mpmk_66_drc"}
